title: The Seven Hermetic Principles: A 49-Day Journey
description: The Kybalion presents seven universal principles said to underlie all existence — from matter to mind, from the cosmos to the inner life. Over 49 days (7 days per principle), you will sit with each one, test it against your experience, and discover how ancient Hermetic wisdom illuminates the deepest patterns of reality.

entries.title: Mentalism — The All Is Mind
entries.intro: We begin with the first and most fundamental of the Hermetic principles: The ALL is Mind; the Universe is Mental. Everything that exists arises from and within infinite, living Mind.

entries.title: The Living Universe
entries.intro: If the universe is mental, then it is alive in the deepest sense. The Hermeticists did not separate matter from spirit — they saw matter as spirit in its densest form. Today we sit with this vision.

entries.title: Mind Within Mind
entries.intro: The Kybalion distinguishes between The All — infinite, unknowable Mind — and the finite minds that exist within it, as thoughts within a thinker. We are not separate from the divine mind; we are expressions of it.

entries.title: Consciousness as Primary
entries.intro: Western materialism inverts the Hermetic view: it sees matter as primary and consciousness as its product. Hermeticism insists on the reverse. Today we explore what changes when we take this seriously.

entries.title: The Power of Thought
entries.intro: If reality is fundamentally mental, then thought is not merely passive reflection — it is creative force. The Hermeticist learns to think carefully, precisely, and deliberately.

entries.title: As Above in Mind
entries.intro: The great Hermetic insight is that the divine mind and the human mind are structurally similar — not identical, but analogous. What we can know of the infinite is revealed through the pattern of our own knowing.

entries.title: Mentalism Integrated
entries.intro: We close the first week. The principle of Mentalism is not a metaphysical curiosity — it is an invitation to take the life of the mind seriously as the primary arena of transformation.

entries.title: Correspondence — As Above, So Below
entries.intro: We begin the second principle: As above, so below; as below, so above. The great axiom of Hermeticism asserts that there is a structural correspondence between all planes of existence.

entries.title: The Three Great Planes
entries.intro: The Kybalion describes three great planes — physical, mental, and spiritual — each reflecting the structure of the others. To understand any one, you illuminate all three.

entries.title: Macrocosm and Microcosm
entries.intro: You are a microcosm of the universe. The same laws that govern galaxies govern cells. The same forces that move empires move the human heart. Today we explore this correspondence.

entries.title: The Inner and the Outer
entries.intro: The principle of correspondence teaches that what we see in the world around us reflects something in the world within. Our outer circumstances are not random — they correspond to inner states.

entries.title: Understanding Through Analogy
entries.intro: Correspondence is the engine of Hermetic understanding. When we encounter something unknown, we find its correspondence in what we know. The invisible is read through the visible.

entries.title: The Pattern Behind the Pattern
entries.intro: Every tradition that has produced genuine insight has discovered that reality is not random — it has structure. Correspondence names this structure and gives us tools to navigate it.

entries.title: Correspondence Integrated
entries.intro: We close the second week. The practical meaning of correspondence is this: to change something on any plane, work on its correspondence on another. Change your thought; change your world.

entries.title: Vibration — Nothing Rests
entries.intro: We begin the third principle: Nothing rests; everything moves; everything vibrates. Modern physics agrees: matter is vibration, energy is vibration, and consciousness is vibration at its highest frequency.

entries.title: The Scale of Vibration
entries.intro: The Hermeticists described a great scale of vibration running from the densest matter to pure spirit. Position on this scale determines the nature of a thing. Higher vibration means greater aliveness.

entries.title: Mental Vibration
entries.intro: Thoughts are vibrations. The Kybalion describes how different mental states — fear, love, courage, despair — correspond to different vibrational frequencies. This is not metaphor: it is mechanism.

entries.title: Entrainment
entries.intro: When two vibrating things are placed together, the one with lower amplitude tends to match the one with higher amplitude. This is why environments, people, and habits matter — they entrain us.

entries.title: Raising the Rate
entries.intro: Every spiritual practice can be understood as an attempt to raise the rate of mental vibration — to move from fear toward love, from contraction toward expansion, from sleep toward wakefulness.

entries.title: The Still Point
entries.intro: The paradox of vibration is that at its highest frequency — beyond which we cannot conceive — it approaches stillness again. The divine mind vibrates at infinite rate, indistinguishable from rest.

entries.title: Vibration Integrated
entries.intro: We close the third week. You are not fixed. Your mental state, your habitual reactions, your relationship to the world — all can be changed, because they are patterns of vibration, not stone.

entries.title: Polarity — Everything Has Its Opposite
entries.intro: We begin the fourth principle: Everything is dual; everything has poles; everything has its pair of opposites. Hot and cold are the same thing — only the degree differs. Light and dark, love and hate, courage and fear.

entries.title: The Spectrum, Not the Binary
entries.intro: Polarity does not mean that opposites are equal — it means they are on the same spectrum. Between complete darkness and blazing light lies every gradation. This changes how we understand our own states.

entries.title: Mental Alchemy
entries.intro: The Kybalion describes the practice of mental transmutation: using the principle of polarity to move deliberately along the spectrum of any mental state. Fear and courage occupy the same pole — only the degree differs.

entries.title: Transcending Polarity
entries.intro: Advanced Hermetic practice moves beyond simply shifting between poles — it seeks the point of equilibrium that transcends both. The sage acts from a place that is neither attached to pleasure nor averse to pain.

entries.title: Paradox as Teacher
entries.intro: Polarity explains why paradox is everywhere in spiritual teaching. 'The way up is the way down.' 'To gain, lose.' 'In dying, we are born.' These are not contradictions — they are descriptions of a single spectrum.

entries.title: Love and Hate
entries.intro: Of all the polarities, love and hate reveal the principle most clearly. They are not opposites in the sense of being categorically different — they are the same intense energy, oriented in different directions.

entries.title: Polarity Integrated
entries.intro: We close the fourth week. Polarity liberates us from the tyranny of binary thinking. Nothing is simply good or bad, high or low, lost or found. Everything is a matter of degree and direction.

entries.title: Rhythm — The Pendulum Swings
entries.intro: We begin the fifth principle: Everything flows, out and in; everything has its tides; all things rise and fall. The pendulum swing manifests in everything — the seasons, the moods, the cycles of history.

entries.title: The Law of Compensation
entries.intro: Every swing to the right is followed by a swing to the left. The measure of the swing is the measure of the return. The Hermeticist learns to read these cycles and prepare accordingly.

entries.title: Rising Above the Swing
entries.intro: Advanced Hermetic practice does not try to stop the pendulum — it cannot be stopped. Instead, the adept learns to raise their consciousness above the plane of the swing, observing without being swept along.

entries.title: Seasons of the Soul
entries.intro: Just as the earth has seasons, so does the life of the soul. Winter is not failure — it is preparation. The period of withdrawal, rest, and apparent stagnation carries its own necessity.

entries.title: Riding the Rhythm
entries.intro: The sailor does not fight the tides — they read them and sail accordingly. The Hermetic student learns the art of working with rhythm rather than against it: acting decisively when the tide runs with them.

entries.title: The Rhythm of Attention
entries.intro: Even attention has its rhythm. Forced, continuous concentration collapses into its opposite. The practice is to work with the natural rhythm of the mind — concentrated effort followed by release.

entries.title: Rhythm Integrated
entries.intro: We close the fifth week. Rhythm is not something that happens to you — it is something you can learn to work with, and ultimately to transcend by rising to the plane from which the swing itself originates.

entries.title: Cause and Effect — Nothing Happens by Chance
entries.intro: We begin the sixth principle: Every cause has its effect; every effect has its cause. Chance is merely a name for an unrecognized cause. Nothing escapes the law.

entries.title: Rising Above the Plane of Effects
entries.intro: Most people live entirely in the realm of effects — buffeted by circumstances they did not choose and do not understand. The Hermetic student learns to trace effects back to causes and to act at the level of cause.

entries.title: Character as Cause
entries.intro: The most powerful causes are not external events but inner states. Our character — our habitual patterns of thought and feeling — is the primary cause generating our experience of life.

entries.title: The Long Chain
entries.intro: Every cause is itself an effect of a prior cause. The chain runs back beyond any single life. The Hermetic practice is not to find the first cause — it is to act wisely within the chain we find ourselves in.

entries.title: Mastery vs. Tools
entries.intro: The Kybalion describes two classes of beings: those who are tools of the law, moved by every wind of circumstance, and those who are Masters — who use the law consciously, at the plane of cause.

entries.title: The Karma of Thought
entries.intro: Before an action is taken, a thought arises. Before a thought arises, a habit of attention. The Hermetic teaching on cause and effect begins, therefore, with mastery of thought.

entries.title: Cause and Effect Integrated
entries.intro: We close the sixth week. The principle of cause and effect is ultimately liberating: if effects have causes, and if we can act at the level of cause, then we are not victims. The game has always been in our hands.

entries.title: Gender — The Creative Principle
entries.intro: We begin the seventh and final principle: Gender is in everything; everything has its masculine and feminine principles. This is not about biology — it is about the universal polarity of receptive and generative energy.

entries.title: Masculine and Feminine as Cosmic Forces
entries.intro: Every creative act requires both principles: the generative impulse and the receptive ground in which it takes form. The Hermeticist recognizes these forces operating at every level — from atoms to galaxies to the mind.

entries.title: The Inner Feminine
entries.intro: The subconscious mind is described by the Kybalion as the feminine pole of mental gender. It receives the impressions given to it by the conscious will — and then gestates and gives them form. Work with it, not against it.

entries.title: The Inner Masculine
entries.intro: The conscious will is the masculine pole — it directs, initiates, and impresses. Its power lies not in force but in precision: the clearly formed intention impressed upon the receptive inner ground.

entries.title: Balance of the Poles
entries.intro: Wisdom requires both poles in proper balance. The will that directs without receiving becomes rigid and blind. The receptivity that receives without directing becomes passive and formless. True mastery cultivates both.

entries.title: Creativity as Sacred Act
entries.intro: When a human being creates — whether a work of art, a new life, a just society, or a transformed self — they are participating in the divine creative act. Gender, in the Hermetic sense, names this mystery.

entries.title: The Seven Principles as One
entries.intro: We arrive at the end. The seven principles are not seven separate laws — they are seven faces of one law. All is mind. All corresponds. All vibrates. All oscillates between poles. All has rhythm. All has cause. All is creative. Sit with the whole.
